token_family_docs.$description: Mode collections — the seven axes along which the design system varies: lightness, emphasis, ui_scale, density, typography_context, motion, viewport. Each collection holds its modes (single values like light / dark / sm / md / xs) and the CSS class string the application adds to activate each mode (the selector). The ui_scale and density collections also carry the multipliers that the active mode applies to base values. The viewport collection also carries the breakpoint primitives and per-range bounds (from / to). In Figma, each mode collection surfaces as a variable collection with one Figma mode per Oblique mode.

Lightness mode collection — user theme preference. Modes: light (the base — no class is applied), dark (.ob-lightness-dark). Each mode carries the CSS class string the application adds to <body> to activate it.

selector.$description: Light is the base lightness mode — no class is applied to <body>. It is the first-paint default; dark overrides it via .ob-lightness-dark.
Developer-facing class. The application adds it to <body> to activate the dark lightness mode, overriding the light base.
